req.title: Close encounter (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall close an Encounter; closed Encounters shall reject further modifications except for addendum Observations. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers the lifecycle transition of an Encounter from 'in-progress' to 'finished', with rejection of further modifications. Five test cases are designed: (1) positive EP/STC – a clinician with valid credentials closes an open encounter and receives status=finished; (2) negative NEG – attempting to modify a closed encounter returns 409; (3) boundary BVA – closing an encounter that is already in the terminal 'finished' state (idempotent-or-conflict boundary); (4) negative DT – a non-clinician (permission-denied) role attempts to close an encounter; (5) negative NEG – closing with a missing mandatory element (encounter ID) to cover missing-field error paths. Together these cover EP, BVA, STC, DT, and NEG heuristics as required.

tests.name: test_clinician_closes_open_encounter_returns_finished
preconditions: Authenticated as a clinician with ENCOUNTER_CLOSE permission
Encounter ID is available via <open_encounter_id> fixture
actions: POST /Encounter/{id}/close with valid clinician auth token
Capture HTTP response status and body
tests.executable:
def test_clinician_closes_open_encounter_returns_finished(open_encounter_id, clinician_auth_headers, api_client):
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/Encounter/{open_encounter_id}/close",
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "finished"

    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Encounter/{open_encounter_id}",
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "finished"

tests.name: test_modify_closed_encounter_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated as a clinician with ENCOUNTER_CLOSE permission
An Encounter exists with status=finished (already closed)
actions: PATCH /Encounter/{id} with a non-addendum modification payload
Capture HTTP response status and error body
expected: Response body contains an error indicating the encounter is already closed
trace: R-HLT-057: closed Encounters shall reject further modifications -> 409
NEG: modification attempt on finished encounter

tests.name: test_close_already_finished_encounter_is_boundary_conflict
preconditions: Authenticated as a clinician with ENCOUNTER_CLOSE permission
An Encounter exists with status=finished (terminal boundary state)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 409 Conflict OR HTTP 200 with idempotent status=finished
If 409: error body indicates invalid state transition
trace: R-HLT-057: conflicting state transitions must be tested
BVA: boundary at finished -> finished (re-close attempt)

tests.name: test_non_clinician_cannot_close_encounter_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated as a user WITHOUT ENCOUNTER_CLOSE permission (e.g. receptionist role)
Non-clinician auth headers available via <non_clinician_auth_headers> fixture
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Encounter status remains in-progress after the rejected request
tests.executable:
def test_non_clinician_cannot_close_encounter_returns_403(open_encounter_id, non_clinician_auth_headers, clinician_auth_headers, api_client):
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/Encounter/{open_encounter_id}/close",
        headers=non_clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403

    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Encounter/{open_encounter_id}",
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "in-progress"

tests.name: test_close_encounter_missing_id_returns_404_or_405
preconditions: Authenticated as a clinician with ENCOUNTER_CLOSE permission
No encounter ID is supplied in the URL (missing mandatory element)
actions: POST /Encounter//close (empty ID segment) with valid clinician auth
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 404 Not Found OR HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed
Response body contains an error; no encounter is modified
trace: R-HLT-057: missing mandatory elements negative path
NEG: mandatory encounter ID absent -> error response
tests.executable:
def test_close_encounter_missing_id_returns_404_or_405(clinician_auth_headers, api_client):
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/Encounter//close",
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (404, 405)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "issue" in body or resp.status_code in (404, 405)
